headline: Forget The Chip Boom: Wall Street's 'Ultimate Contrarian Trade' Favors Software For 2026
summary: Technology sector leaders across software and semiconductors are approaching a potential inflection point, as BTIG Chief Market Technician Jonathan Krinsky outlines a contrarian shift favoring software over chip stocks. Extreme Valuation Gap Signals Reversal Setup Krinsky said the spread between software stocks in the S&P 1500 and semiconductor stocks in the SOX index has reached an unprecedented level, with the ratio falling 43% below its 200-day moving average. headline: Assessing Palantir Technologies (PLTR) After Recent Swings In Share Price And AI Contract Momentum
summary: If you are wondering whether Palantir Technologies at US$146.39 is still good value after a strong run, the starting point is to separate the story from the price you see on screen. The stock has returned 14.3% over the last 7 days, while the 30 day return is a 2.8% decline, the year to date return is a 12.8% decline and the 1 year return sits at 56.1%, with a very large 3 year gain and a 5 year return of more than 5x. headline: Prediction: The AI Supply Chain Shortage Will Create 2 New Trillion-Dollar Companies by 2030
summary: AMD and Oracle are important players in the AI infrastructure space, and they are likely to clock impressive earnings growth through the end of the decade.
